documents.name: Arizona Christian Tuition Project Service Agreement
documents.content:
SERVICE AGREEMENT
Constructed Matter – Website, CRM, Automation & Design Services
Effective Date: January 19, 2026 (or upon execution)
This Service Agreement (“Agreement”) is entered into by and between:

1. Parties
1.1 Service Provider
Qallus, LLC
Attn: Jeremy Waters - Owner
Email: jwaters@qallus.co
Mobile: (480) 352-7598
Office: (480) 999-9906


1.2 Client
Arizona Christian Tuition
Attn: Chris Leavitt – President / Principal
Email: chris@arizonachristiantuition.com
Phone: (480) 
Attn: Scott Spaulding – Principal / Construction Manager
Email: scott@arizonachristiantuition.com
Phone: (480) 


SERVICE AGREEMENT
Strategic Services, Technology, and Revenue Participation Agreement
This Service Agreement (“Agreement”) is entered into as of [Effective Date], by and between:
Arizona Christian Tuition, a [State] organization, with its principal place of business at [Address] (“Client”),
 and
 Jeremy Waters / [Your Legal Business Entity Name], with its principal place of business at [Address] (“Service Provider”).
Client and Service Provider may be referred to individually as a “Party” and collectively as the “Parties.”

1. PURPOSE OF AGREEMENT
Client has engaged Service Provider to design, develop, manage, and maintain its brand, digital infrastructure, technology systems, marketing assets, and operational platforms, including but not limited to web, CRM, automation, communications, and print and digital media.
In lieu of traditional monetary compensation, the Parties have agreed to an alternative compensation structure as outlined in this Agreement.

2. SCOPE OF SERVICES
Service Provider has provided and will continue to provide, as requested and reasonably required, services including but not limited to:
2.1 Brand & Creative Services
Brand identity development and management


Graphic design for print and digital assets


Marketing collateral and visual systems


2.2 Web & Application Development
Website design, development, hosting, and maintenance


Web application and platform development


User experience (UX) and user interface (UI) design


Ongoing updates, enhancements, and bug fixes


2.3 CRM & Automation
CRM setup, configuration, and management


Workflow automation (including integrations with third-party tools)


Lead management, donor management, and reporting


2.4 Communications Infrastructure
SMS and voice systems (including Twilio)


Email systems and deliverability management


Notification, messaging, and automation logic


2.5 Hosting & Infrastructure
VPS hosting and server management


Domain configuration and DNS management


Security, uptime monitoring, and backups


2.6 Third-Party Tools & APIs
Integration and management of third-party platforms, APIs, and services


Configuration and maintenance of external tools required for operations


2.7 Ongoing Management & Support
System oversight and optimization


Troubleshooting and technical support


Strategic guidance related to digital and operational systems



3. COMPENSATION & CONSIDERATION
3.1 Tuition Coverage (Primary Consideration)
In full or partial consideration for the services rendered:
Client agrees to cover the full private school education expenses for Service Provider’s children through graduation.


Tuition coverage shall include all required tuition, mandatory fees, and standard educational costs charged by the institution(s).


Tuition obligations shall continue regardless of Client revenue performance.


3.2 Revenue Participation (Secondary Consideration)
If Client generates revenue from any source, including but not limited to:
Donations


Fundraising


Memberships


Events


Sponsorships


Platform usage


Any other monetization activities


Client agrees to pay Service Provider a revenue participation fee equal to:
_____ % of gross revenue
(Percentage intentionally left blank and to be mutually agreed upon in writing.)
Payments shall be calculated and remitted on a [monthly / quarterly] basis with supporting revenue documentation.

4. NO UPFRONT FEES; FAIR VALUE ACKNOWLEDGMENT
Client acknowledges that:
Service Provider has not charged any upfront monetary fees for services rendered to date.


The services provided represent substantial professional value, including time, expertise, infrastructure, and financial cost.


This Agreement exists to ensure Service Provider is fairly compensated for that value.



5. FAILURE TO PERFORM / CLIENT DEFAULT
5.1 Tuition or Revenue Default
If Client fails to:
Pay or maintain tuition coverage, or


Pay agreed revenue participation amounts,


Client shall become financially liable for compensation to Service Provider.
5.2 Compensation Upon Default
Upon default, Client agrees to compensate Service Provider for:
All time invested (billed at $____ / hour or fair market rate)


Hosting and infrastructure costs


VPS resources


Twilio usage (SMS, voice, messaging)


Software licenses


Third-party services


Development, design, and management services


Any out-of-pocket expenses incurred on Client’s behalf


Service Provider may issue an invoice, which shall be due within 30 days of receipt.

6. OWNERSHIP & INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY
6.1 Conditional Ownership
All work product, code, designs, systems, and assets created by Service Provider remain the property of Service Provider unless and until:
Client is in good standing with all obligations under this Agreement.


6.2 License to Use
While in good standing, Client is granted a non-exclusive, revocable license to use the deliverables for its operations.
6.3 Reversion of Rights
In the event of default or termination for cause, Service Provider reserves the right to:
Revoke licenses


Suspend services


Retain ownership of all intellectual property



7. THIRD-PARTY SERVICES & LIMITATION OF LIABILITY
Client acknowledges that systems may rely on third-party services including but not limited to:
Hosting providers


Email providers


SMS and voice platforms


APIs and software tools


Service Provider is not responsible for outages, changes, or failures caused by third-party platforms.

8. CONFIDENTIALITY
Both Parties agree to maintain confidentiality of proprietary, technical, and sensitive information shared during the course of this Agreement.

9. INDEMNIFICATION
Client agrees to indemnify and hold harmless Service Provider from any claims, damages, losses, or liabilities arising from:
Client’s use of the systems


Client’s content or communications


Client’s legal or regulatory obligations



10. TERMINATION
Either Party may terminate this Agreement with written notice.
Termination does not relieve Client of:
Tuition obligations already incurred


Revenue participation owed


Compensation owed due to default



11. GOVERNING LAW
This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of Arizona.

12. ENTIRE AGREEMENT
This Agreement constitutes the entire understanding between the Parties and supersedes all prior agreements, written or oral.

13. SIGNATURES
Service Provider
 Name: __________________________
 Signature: ______________________
 Date: ___________________________
Client (Arizona Christian Tuition)
 Name: __________________________
 Title: __________________________
 Signature: ______________________
 Date: ___________________________






24. Acceptance & Signatures
By signing below, the parties agree to the terms of this Agreement.

Date:                           Print:                                             Signature:

Attn: Brandon Fadden – President / Principal
Email: brandon@constructedmatter.com
Phone: (480) 628-4458
____________________________________________________________________________

Date:                          Print:                                             Signature:


Attn: Joseph Ballard – Principal / Construction Manager
Email: joe@constructedmatter.com
Phone: (480) 628-4458
____________________________________________________________________________
Date:                          Print:                                             Signature:

Jeremy Waters, Qallus LLC
jwaters@qallus.co 
Phone: (480) 352-7598
